diff --git crates/tsz-checker/tests/union_source_structural_elaboration_tests.rs crates/tsz-checker/tests/union_source_structural_elaboration_tests.rs
index 5b00d85b0f..5e59ee20c8 100644
--- crates/tsz-checker/tests/union_source_structural_elaboration_tests.rs
+++ crates/tsz-checker/tests/union_source_structural_elaboration_tests.rs
@@ -61,6 +61,14 @@ fn chain_has<P: Fn(&str) -> bool>(chain: &[(u8, u32, String)], depth: u8, predic
         .any(|(d, _, msg)| *d == depth && predicate(msg))
 }
 
+/// Whether any elaboration line (at any depth) is the leaf relation
+/// `Type '<from>' is not assignable to type '<to>'`.
+fn chain_has_leaf(chain: &[(u8, u32, String)], from: &str, to: &str) -> bool {
+    chain
+        .iter()
+        .any(|(_, _, m)| m.contains(&format!("Type '{from}' is not assignable to type '{to}'")))
+}
+
 /// Tuple union member failing on an element type mismatch elaborates the member
 /// header, the TS2626 positional line, and the leaf relation, each one indent
 /// deeper than the last.
@@ -195,6 +203,94 @@ const t: Target = s;
     );
 }
 
+/// Nullish member priority matches `tsc`'s relation order. `tsc`'s
+/// `eachTypeRelatedToType` walks the source union in ascending *type-id* order,
+/// where the intrinsic `undefined` and `null` types (allocated before any user
+/// type) sort first — so when a nullish member and a non-nullish member both
+/// fail, `tsc` elaborates the *nullish* one, regardless of where it appears in
+/// the union's *printed* order (which keeps `undefined`/`null` last). tsz stores
+/// union members in that printer order, so without restoring the relation order
+/// it would elaborate the non-nullish member (`number`) instead of the nullish
+/// one (`undefined`/`null`).
+#[test]
+fn union_member_selection_prioritizes_nullish_like_tsc() {
+    // `number | undefined` -> `string`: both members fail; tsc reports `undefined`.
+    let undef = ts2322_chain(&diagnostics(
+        r#"
+declare const x: number | undefined;
+const y: string = x;
+"#,
+    ));
+    assert!(
+        chain_has_leaf(&undef, "undefined", "string"),
+        "expected the `undefined` member to be elaborated; got {undef:?}"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        !chain_has_leaf(&undef, "number", "string"),
+        "the non-nullish `number` member must not be the elaborated member \
+         when `undefined` is present; got {undef:?}"
+    );
+
+    // Reversed print/source order must not change the pick — still `undefined`.
+    let undef_rev = ts2322_chain(&diagnostics(
+        r#"
+declare const x: undefined | number;
+const y: string = x;
+"#,
+    ));
+    assert!(
+        chain_has_leaf(&undef_rev, "undefined", "string")
+            && !chain_has_leaf(&undef_rev, "number", "string"),
+        "reversed union must still elaborate `undefined`; got {undef_rev:?}"
+    );
+
+    // With no `undefined`, `null` takes priority over the non-nullish member.
+    let null_chain = ts2322_chain(&diagnostics(
+        r#"
+declare const x: number | null;
+const y: string = x;
+"#,
+    ));
+    assert!(
+        chain_has_leaf(&null_chain, "null", "string")
+            && !chain_has_leaf(&null_chain, "number", "string"),
+        "expected the `null` member to be elaborated; got {null_chain:?}"
+    );
+
+    // When both `null` and `undefined` are present, `undefined` (the earlier
+    // intrinsic) wins over `null`, and both win over the non-nullish member.
+    let both = ts2322_chain(&diagnostics(
+        r#"
+declare const x: null | undefined | boolean;
+const y: string = x;
+"#,
+    ));
+    assert!(
+        chain_has_leaf(&both, "undefined", "string")
+            && !chain_has_leaf(&both, "null", "string")
+            && !chain_has_leaf(&both, "boolean", "string"),
+        "expected `undefined` to win over `null` and the non-nullish member; got {both:?}"
+    );
+}
+
+/// A failing nullish member that is *assignable* to the target must be skipped,
+/// falling through to the first failing non-nullish member — matching tsc.
+#[test]
+fn union_member_selection_skips_assignable_nullish_member() {
+    // `null` is assignable to `string | null`, so the elaboration must drill the
+    // non-nullish `number` member rather than the (assignable) `null` member.
+    let chain = ts2322_chain(&diagnostics(

content.content:
1180	        // Union source: the relation failed, so at least one member is not
1181	        // assignable to the target. tsc keeps the root mismatch visible by
1182	        // elaborating the first failing member beneath the union-to-target line
1183	        // (`Type 'A | B' is not assignable to type 'T'.` -> `Type 'B' is not
1184	        // assignable to type 'T'.`). Without this, the chain stops at the bare
1185	        // union line and hides why the assignment fails (e.g. the `undefined`
1186	        // member contributed by an optional property).
1187	        //
1188	        // Prefer the evaluated `resolved_source` when it is still a union (e.g. a
1189	        // conditional/mapped type that evaluates *into* a union). Otherwise fall
1190	        // back to `pre_eval_source`: when subtype reduction during `evaluate_type`
1191	        // collapsed a written union of structurally-related members (e.g.
1192	        // `string[] | [string, string]` -> `string[]`), the member elaboration
1193	        // must still walk the members tsc preserves under `UnionReduction.Literal`.
1194	        let (union_member_source, union_member_list) =
1195	            match union_list_id(self.interner, resolved_source) {
1196	                Some(list) => (resolved_source, Some(list)),
1197	                None => (
1198	                    pre_eval_source,
1199	                    union_list_id(self.interner, pre_eval_source),
1200	                ),
1201	            };
1202	        if let Some(member_list) = union_member_list {
1203	            let members = self.interner.type_list(member_list);
1204	            for &member in members.iter() {
1205	                if member == source || member == union_member_source {
1206	                    // Defensive: avoid self-recursion on a degenerate union.
1207	                    continue;
1208	                }
1209	                if self.check_subtype(member, target).is_true() {
1210	                    continue;
1211	                }
1212	                // Elaborate the first failing member beneath the union-to-target
1213	                // line, mirroring tsc which always drills into that member. Each
1214	                // arm below is a member-failure reason whose render composes
1215	                // under the union line:
1216	                //   * leaf relations, property summaries
1217	                //     (`MissingProperty`/`MissingProperties`), and the
1218	                //     array-element and readonly-to-mutable reasons self-head —
1219	                //     their rendered line already names the member
1220	                //     (`Property 'a' is missing in type '{ b: 2; }' …`,
1221	                //     `Type 'number[]' is not assignable to type 'string[]' …`,
1222	                //     `The type 'readonly [number]' is 'readonly' …`).
1223	                //   * the tuple/property element-type, index-signature, and
1224	                //     function-return mismatches are header-led; the union-source
1225	                //     renderer supplies the `Type 'M' is not assignable to type
1226	                //     'T'.` member header before drilling (`Type at position 0 …`
1227	                //     / `Types of property 'p' …` / `'string' index signatures are
1228	                //     incompatible.` / the bare return-relation leaf).
1229	                //   * `ParameterTypeMismatch` self-heads with the signature
1230	                //     relation line at depth >= 1, so the renderer routes it
1231	                //     through the self-heading path (its own first line doubles as
1232	                //     the member header), then drills `Types of parameters 'a' and
1233	                //     'b' are incompatible.` + the contravariant leaf.
1234	                // Without this the chain collapses to the bare union-to-target
1235	                // line and hides which member fails.
1236	                //
1237	                // The set is intentionally limited to member-failure shapes whose
1238	                // render composes exactly with `tsc` here.
1239	                //
1240	                // Tuple fixed-arity (`TupleElementMismatch`) and variadic-arity
1241	                // (`TupleArityMismatch`) count mismatches are header-led leaves:
1242	                // their `TS2618`/`TS2619` (`Source has N element(s) but target
1243	                // requires/allows only M`) text carries no member name, so the
1244	                // union renderer supplies the `Type 'M' is not assignable to type
1245	                // 'T'.` member header (via `union_member_nested_needs_header`)
1246	                // before drilling the arity leaf — matching tsc:
1247	                //   Type '[2, 3] | [4]' is not assignable to type '[number]'.
1248	                //     Type '[2, 3]' is not assignable to type '[number]'.
1249	                //       Source has 2 element(s) but target allows only 1.
1250	                //
1251	                // Notably excluded:
1252	                //   * `OptionalPropertyRequired`/`ReadonlyPropertyMismatch` — `tsc`
1253	                //     leads these with the member header (and they only arise in
1254	                //     narrow `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` / readonly-index shapes),
1255	                //     so they need the header-led path, not this self-heading one.
1256	                let nested = self.explain_failure_guarded(member, target);
1257	                if let Some(nested) = nested
1258	                    && matches!(
1259	                        nested,
1260	                        SubtypeFailureReason::TypeMismatch { .. }
1261	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::IntrinsicTypeMismatch { .. }
1262	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::LiteralTypeMismatch { .. }
1263	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::MissingProperty { .. }
1264	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::MissingProperties { .. }
1265	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::TupleElementTypeMismatch { .. }
1266	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::TupleElementMismatch { .. }
1267	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::TupleArityMismatch(_)
1268	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::TupleVariadicPositionMismatch { .. }
1269	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::PropertyTypeMismatch { .. }
1270	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::ArrayElementMismatch { .. }
1271	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::IndexSignatureMismatch { .. }
1272	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::ReturnTypeMismatch { .. }
1273	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::ParameterTypeMismatch { .. }
1274	                            | SubtypeFailureReason::ReadonlyToMutableAssignment { .. }
1275	                    )
1276	                {
1277	                    return Some(SubtypeFailureReason::UnionSourceMismatch {
1278	                        source_type: source,
1279	                        target_type: target,
1280	                        member_type: member,
1281	                        nested_reason: Box::new(nested),
1282	                    });
1283	                }
1284	                break;
1285	            }
1286	        }

1288	        // Conditional types that survived `evaluate_type` (i.e. deferred
1289	        // conditionals like `T extends U ? X : Y` where `T` is a type
1290	        // parameter) are not handled by any structural shape arm above and
1291	        // would otherwise collapse to the bare `TypeMismatch` fallback,
1292	        // hiding the actual branch-level relation failure.
1293	        //
1294	        // The structural rule, applicable on either side: a relation
1295	        // involving a deferred conditional fails exactly when at least one
1296	        // of its branches fails the corresponding branch relation. Surface
1297	        // that failing branch as a `ConditionalBranchMismatch` carrying the
1298	        // nested branch reason so the diagnostic chain stays intact.

content.text:
All 21 tests pass.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The root cause was an ordering mismatch, not a logic error: tsz stores union members in printer-friendly order (nullish types last, matching how they display in `A | B | undefined`), but tsc's internal `eachTypeRelatedToType` walks unions in *type-id allocation* order, where `undefined`/`null` are intrinsic types allocated very early. Since the union-source elaboration code picked the *first* failing member and stopped there, the two orderings produced different diagnostics for the same source code.

The fix reorders only the iteration used for elaboration selection (not the underlying `TypeList`, which other code — like display/printing — still relies on staying in its original order). That's a good general pattern: when two different consumers of the same data structure need different orderings, sort locally at the point of use rather than mutating the shared representation.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Fixed `crates/tsz-solver/src/relations/subtype/explain.rs`: union-source member elaboration now prioritizes `undefined`, then `null`, before other members when picking which failing member to drill into, matching `tsc`'s type-id-ordered relation walk instead of tsz's printer-ordered union list.
